articles.title: The Mona Lisa Vanished! And a Broadway Star Is Turning the Heist into a Musical
articles.summary: Imagine the most famous painting in the world just disappearing one day. That's what happened to the Mona Lisa in 1911! Now, Andrew Lloyd Webber, the guy who wrote 'The Phantom of the Opera' and 'Cats,' is writing a musical about the crazy true story. The thief was a worker at the Louvre museum who hid the painting in his trunk for two years. When police finally found it, the painting became a worldwide superstar. Get ready for songs about stolen art!

articles.title: Black-and-White Photos That Feel Like a Warm Hug: Meet Austn Fischer
articles.summary: Photographer Austn Fischer takes black-and-white pictures that feel surprisingly warm and soft. He loves mixing weird things together, like a fancy lace collar with sporty clothes. He says he uses photos to understand his own feelings. Recently, he even spent two weeks painting a wall white over and over to learn patience! His pictures have appeared in big magazines, and he's shot famous people like artist Ai Weiwei. His work is all about how we use clothes and poses to show who we are.

articles.title: A Giant Buddha Rises Again on the High Line — Made from War Metal!
articles.summary: A 27-foot-tall sandstone Buddha has appeared on New York City's High Line park. But it's not just any statue. It's a recreation of the ancient Bamiyan Buddhas that were destroyed by the Taliban in 2001. The artist, Tuan Andrew Nguyen, did something amazing: he made the statue's hands from melted-down artillery (weapons) from Afghanistan! The right hand makes a 'fearlessness' gesture, and the left shows compassion. The statue is called 'The Light That Shines Through the Universe.' It will be on view until spring 2027.
